Healthy, productive apple tree. Full of ripe fruit (but few ground falls) in early Sept. Apples are crisp and slightly tart, like a cross between a Braeburn and Granny Smith.

It's in the parking lot shared by a Wendy's and self-storage place, partially over
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Loaded apple tree in the middle of the walmart/at home parking lot. Unpruned, so the apples are a bit small. But mostly look good. Not quite ripe yet in mid August 2025 Access: Public.
Some very nice crabapples to be had at the cemetery. Neighbors guaranteed not to bother you as you pick!
Two Beautiful Crab Apple Trees at the PDQ on Mineral Point and Randolph.
Walnut Grove Park. Crabapples in no-mow area, one near path on left by soccer field, 2 near shelter--all have small green berries. 202 N Westfield Rd, Madison, WI 53717
Fairly tart apples growing from this tree

Blackberry brambles along both sides of this section of the trail, as well as copious amounts of burdock and garlic mustard. 

This is a conservancy, so permission is needed to pick. Access: Private.

A line of a dozen or so elderberry bushes along the trail. Also lots of Queen Anne's Lace.
